items.note: Filter-focused cone baseline for J-Max S from community filter conversions.
Cone-focused baseline for J-Ultra using turn notation so baristas can map from espresso-oriented dial habits.
Flat-bottom and immersion recipes on J-Ultra generally run slightly coarser than cone baseline to avoid clogging.
Curated JE-Plus filter range using external-number indexing; always confirm burr zero before relying on numeric transfer.
Flat-bottom reference drifts slightly coarser than cone baseline to avoid drawdown stall on denser beds.
JX-Pro S pour-over baseline from external-number indexing; always zero the burr before transferring settings.
Flat-bed and immersion recipes usually run a touch coarser than the JX-Pro S cone baseline.
K-Max medium reference anchored to K-series numbering conventions for filter-first use.
Flat-bed and immersion baseline for K-Max, tuned slightly coarser than cone references for stable drawdown.
Flat-bed baseline for K-Plus tuned slightly coarser than cone references.
Official 1Zpresso reference baseline for pour over style brewing; adjust slightly finer for iced and slightly coarser for larger flat-bottom beds only after taste.
Use the pour-over baseline and drift slightly coarser for flat-bottom or immersion-style beds.
Cone-focused Q Air/Q2 baseline for V60 and origami style brews with tighter clarity targeting.
Flat and immersion baseline for X-Pro/X-Ultra with a slightly coarser target than cone references.
Official ZP6 pour-over reference favors clarity-focused grind spacing.
Flat-bottom brewers usually want the ZP6 just a touch coarser than cone recipes.
Home filter baseline for the Smart Grinder Pro; burr zero and roast level still matter.

Community-verified filter baseline; absolute click depends on zero point.
Go slightly coarser than cone recipes for flatter beds or immersion release brewers.
Red Clix mapping gives finer-granularity notation for C40 users who track doubled click counts.
Cone-first C60 reference for pour-over clarity before moving to broader flat-bed bands.
One-line mapping for cafe handover between cone and flat recipes on X25 without switching grinder notation style.
Trailmaster filter baseline aligned to C40 geometry with slightly wider travel tolerance.
Cone-first range for Mini Slim with practical home-barista click counting from burr lock.
Flat-bottom and immersion range for Mini Slim where slightly coarser settings reduce over-extraction risk.
HARIO Smart G baseline for daily filter use in home workflows; adjust by cup clarity before tightening grind.
HARIO Smart G flat-bed baseline biased coarser to reduce stall risk on dense paper beds.
Cone baseline for K0/K1 aligned with manual pour-over flow control.
Turn-equivalent mapping helps baristas move between click-count and rotation workflows on K2.
K2 medium band baseline for manual filter workflow; verify burr zero before transfer.
Cone baseline for K3 with medium-light roast service range.
Turn-equivalent filter range for K4 keeps handoff consistent across dial notation styles.
K4 filter baseline mirrors medium hand-grinder spacing; confirm burr lock and zero point first.
Community-verified pour-over baseline; confirm zero point first.
Flat-bottom brewers tend to prefer the K6 slightly coarser than cone baselines.
Kinu M47 cone baseline for clearer pour-over profiles, assuming burr zero is locked consistently.
Kinu M47 flat-bottom and immersion range to preserve flow and avoid over-extraction at dense doses.
Flat-bottom and immersion baseline for Kinu Phoenix with drawdown-safe range.
Cone baseline for Timemore C2 after burr-zero calibration; start here for V60-style and tighten only after cup feedback.

C3 Max filter baseline aligned with C3 platform step geometry for manual brewers.
C5 series baseline anchored to manufacturer positioning plus curated filter references.

Flat-bottom and immersion brewers generally run slightly coarser than cone settings on C5 Pro.
Electric filter baseline for Sculptor 078S across cone and flat workflows.
Click-equivalent mapping mirrors Exagrind turn references for faster recipe sharing in service.
Curated cone baseline for the 600N platform used across Feima / Latina / Flying Eagle rebrands; verify burr zero and burr seasoning before shifting finer.
Flat-bottom and immersion recipes on the 600N platform generally run coarser than cone references to keep drawdown stable and avoid muddiness.
